title: Cross-Framework Pipeline
constraints: The pipeline must use at least two different frameworks
Data must flow correctly between framework boundaries
Each framework must handle its own stage of the pipeline
The output of one framework's stage must be usable as input to the next
hints: Extract the string output from one framework before passing it as input to the next
Use CrewAI for the research stage and LangGraph for the processing/decision stage
Define a clear interface (plain string or dict) at each framework boundary

starterCode.openai-agents-sdk:
from agents import Agent, Runner

# BUG: Entire pipeline runs in a single framework
# TODO: Split into a cross-framework pipeline

researcher = Agent(
    name="Researcher",
    instructions="You are a research analyst. Gather comprehensive data on the given topic.",
)

processor = Agent(
    name="Processor",
    instructions="You are a data processor. Organize and structure research findings.",
)

advisor = Agent(
    name="Advisor",
    instructions="You are a decision advisor. Make recommendations based on processed data.",
)

research = Runner.run_sync(researcher, "Research: Best cloud provider for AI workloads")
processed = Runner.run_sync(processor, f"Process this research: {research.final_output}")
decision = Runner.run_sync(advisor, f"Based on this analysis, provide recommendations: {processed.final_output}")
print(decision.final_output)
starterCode.langgraph:
from langchain_openai import ChatOpenAI
from langgraph.graph import StateGraph, START, END
from typing import TypedDict

llm = ChatOpenAI(model="gpt-4o-mini")

class State(TypedDict):
    topic: str
    research: str
    analysis: str
    recommendation: str

# BUG: All stages use the same framework
# TODO: Integrate stages from different frameworks
def research(state: State) -> dict:
    response = llm.invoke(f"Research: {state['topic']}")
    return {"research": response.content}

def process(state: State) -> dict:
    response = llm.invoke(f"Process this research: {state['research']}")
    return {"analysis": response.content}

def decide(state: State) -> dict:
    response = llm.invoke(f"Provide recommendations based on: {state['analysis']}")
    return {"recommendation": response.content}

graph = StateGraph(State)
graph.add_node("research", research)
graph.add_node("process", process)
graph.add_node("decide", decide)
graph.add_edge(START, "research")
graph.add_edge("research", "process")
graph.add_edge("process", "decide")
graph.add_edge("decide", END)

app = graph.compile()
result = app.invoke({"topic": "Best cloud provider for AI workloads", "research": "", "analysis": "", "recommendation": ""})
print(result["recommendation"])
starterCode.crewai:
from crewai import Agent, Task, Crew, Process
from crewai import LLM

llm = LLM(model="gpt-4o-mini")

# BUG: Entire pipeline runs in CrewAI only
# TODO: Use CrewAI for research and another framework for processing/decisions

researcher = Agent(
    role="Researcher",
    goal="Gather comprehensive data on the topic",
    backstory="You are a senior research analyst.",
    llm=llm,
)

processor = Agent(
    role="Processor",
    goal="Organize and structure research findings",
    backstory="You are a data processing specialist.",
    llm=llm,
)

advisor = Agent(
    role="Advisor",
    goal="Make recommendations based on processed data",
    backstory="You are a strategic decision advisor.",
    llm=llm,
)

research_task = Task(
    description="Research: {topic}",
    expected_output="Comprehensive research findings",
    agent=researcher,
)

process_task = Task(
    description="Process and organize the research findings",
    expected_output="Structured analysis",
    agent=processor,
)

decision_task = Task(
    description="Provide strategic recommendations",
    expected_output="Actionable recommendations",
    agent=advisor,
)

crew = Crew(
    agents=[researcher, processor, advisor],
    tasks=[research_task, process_task, decision_task],
    process=Process.sequential,
)

result = crew.kickoff(inputs={"topic": "Best cloud provider for AI workloads"})
print(result)

evaluationCriteria.label: Uses multiple frameworks
evaluationCriteria.description: The pipeline uses at least two different agent frameworks

evaluationCriteria.label: Data flows across boundaries
evaluationCriteria.description: Output from one framework's stage correctly feeds into the next framework's stage

evaluationCriteria.label: Each framework handles its stage
evaluationCriteria.description: Each framework is responsible for a distinct stage of the pipeline
